strategy_rationale: Switch the learner from a linear two-model logistic regression to a non-linear class-transformation gradient-boosting estimator. The semantic rationale is that tree ensembles can pick up threshold and interaction effects among the same RFM, basket and points variables (e.g., sharp recency cut-offs, redemption-lag × basket-depth interactions) that a linear model cannot, thereby reducing the under-fitting observed in the first trial. Feature semantics: The current uplift signal is weak (AUC≈0.045) and XAI hints that age is over-represented. human_semantic_v1 adds audited recency-frequency, points accrual, redemption-lag and basket mix features collected strictly up to the campaign-issue reference date. This richer post-issue behavioural snapshot can expose treatment-heterogeneity that the RFM baseline misses while still avoiding look-ahead leakage. | Expected: Higher uplift-AUC and a Qini curve whose top drivers are behavioural recency, points burn-rate and basket depth rather than the previously dominant age feature, signalling that richer behaviour-based heterogeneity has been captured. Expected feature signal: Higher uplift-AUC and a Qini curve showing separation driven by behavioural recency, points burn-rate and basket depth instead of pure demographics.
feature_semantics_rationale: The current uplift signal is weak (AUC≈0.045) and XAI hints that age is over-represented. human_semantic_v1 adds audited recency-frequency, points accrual, redemption-lag and basket mix features collected strictly up to the campaign-issue reference date. This richer post-issue behavioural snapshot can expose treatment-heterogeneity that the RFM baseline misses while still avoiding look-ahead leakage.
feature_expected_signal: Higher uplift-AUC and a Qini curve showing separation driven by behavioural recency, points burn-rate and basket depth instead of pure demographics.
judge_narrative: The trial’s hold-out uplift-AUC fell from 0.04667 (baseline) to 0.04245 and Qini-AUC from 309.80 to 294.67, so it fails to beat the existing model.
policy_narrative: The 10% cutoff yields the highest (least negative) ROI among tested segments, although still unprofitable.

strategy_rationale: First trial that (1) drops the three demographic columns and (2) switches to the hybrid_safe_semantic_v1 recipe, which injects audited recency, frequency, points earn/burn ratios, redemption lag and basket-mix counts while still respecting the safe_history_until_reference temporal cut. These changes should force the learner to rely on behavioural rather than cohort features. Feature semantics: RFM-only trials show weak uplift signal (AUC≈0.045) and XAI dominated by age. human_semantic_v1 injects audited recency, frequency, points-earn/burn ratios, redemption lag and basket-mix counts, all cut at the campaign issue date, giving the model the behavioural resolution needed to surface treatment heterogeneity while still honouring the same temporal cut-off used in production scoring. | Expected: Held-out Qini should exceed the current best 309.8 and uplift-AUC should rise, with XAI now highlighting recency, points burn-rate and basket depth in the top drivers instead of a single age feature. Expected feature signal: Higher held-out Qini (>309.8) driven by behavioural features; uplift-AUC rises; XAI report shows recency, points burn-rate and basket depth in the top drivers instead of a single age feature.
feature_semantics_rationale: RFM-only trials show weak uplift signal (AUC≈0.045) and XAI dominated by age. human_semantic_v1 injects audited recency, frequency, points-earn/burn ratios, redemption lag and basket-mix counts, all cut at the campaign issue date, giving the model the behavioural resolution needed to surface treatment heterogeneity while still honouring the same temporal cut-off used in production scoring.
feature_expected_signal: Higher held-out Qini (>309.8) driven by behavioural features; uplift-AUC rises; XAI report shows recency, points burn-rate and basket depth in the top drivers instead of a single age feature.
judge_narrative: The trial’s held-out uplift AUC rose from 0.0467 to 0.0580 (+24%), and Qini AUC edged up from 309.8 to 310.2, with uplift@5% improving to 0.1315. These consistent gains in uplift metrics confirm that removing demographic features and adding behavioural signals increased true treatment effect separability.
policy_narrative: The 10 % cutoff still yields positive ROI (0.15) while capturing more incremental conversions and revenue than the tighter 5 % segment.

strategy_rationale: First trial that pairs class_transformation with XGBoost on the proven hybrid_safe_semantic_v1 behavioural recipe. Semantically, the recipe filters all behavioural history at safe_history_until_reference, preventing look-ahead leakage and letting recency, frequency, points burn-rate and basket depth express heterogeneous treatment effects while keeping age secondary. Feature semantics: The last run proved that hybrid_safe_semantic_v1 gives the strongest held-out Qini (310.2) and the highest uplift-AUC (0.064) while finally knocking age out of the top-driver list. The recipe only draws behaviour up to the campaign-issue reference date through the builder’s safe_history_until_reference filter, so it respects the production scoring cut-off and eliminates look-ahead leakage. It therefore matches the runtime dataset exactly and keeps the search focused on behavioural heterogeneity rather than demographic propensity. | Expected: Higher held-out Qini and uplift-AUC (target ≥ 310.2 and ≥ 0.064 respectively). XAI should show behavioural recency/frequency/points burn-rate and basket depth among the top drivers, with age not dominating. Expected feature signal: Replicated or higher held-out Qini and uplift-AUC with XAI showing recency, frequency, points burn-rate and basket depth among the leading drivers; age should stay secondary.
feature_semantics_rationale: The last run proved that hybrid_safe_semantic_v1 gives the strongest held-out Qini (310.2) and the highest uplift-AUC (0.064) while finally knocking age out of the top-driver list. The recipe only draws behaviour up to the campaign-issue reference date through the builder’s safe_history_until_reference filter, so it respects the production scoring cut-off and eliminates look-ahead leakage. It therefore matches the runtime dataset exactly and keeps the search focused on behavioural heterogeneity rather than demographic propensity.
feature_expected_signal: Replicated or higher held-out Qini and uplift-AUC with XAI showing recency, frequency, points burn-rate and basket depth among the leading drivers; age should stay secondary.
judge_narrative: The new model outperforms the prior champion on held-out uplift metrics: Qini AUC rises from 310.21 to 325.98 and uplift-AUC from 0.058026 to 0.058876, with perfect stability (1.0), confirming improved uplift performance.
policy_narrative: The 5% threshold delivers the only positive ROI (0.256) with a healthy incremental revenue lift, while broader cut-offs drive ROI negative.

strategy_rationale: First LightGBM run on this feature recipe; leverages the same audited behavioural variables (recency / frequency / points / basket-mix) that previously removed age from the top-driver list, ensuring no temporal leakage (safe_history_until_reference). Feature semantics: This recipe already delivered the strongest held-out Qini (326) and uplift-AUC (≈0.060–0.064) while knocking age out of the top-driver list. It builds only from customer behaviour that occurred on or before the campaign-issue reference date, exactly matching the data cut used at runtime, so there is no look-ahead leakage. Because it layers audited recency, frequency, points earn/burn, redemption-lag and basket-mix variables on top of the basic RFM set, it gives the model richer behavioural heterogeneity signals than the demographic-heavy baseline. | Expected: Held-out Qini ≥ 326 and uplift-AUC ≈ 0.065 with XAI showing recency, points burn-rate and basket depth as top drivers while age stays secondary. Expected feature signal: Held-out Qini should remain ≥326 and uplift-AUC should rise toward or above 0.065, with XAI showing recency, points burn-rate and basket depth among the top drivers while age stays secondary.
feature_semantics_rationale: This recipe already delivered the strongest held-out Qini (326) and uplift-AUC (≈0.060–0.064) while knocking age out of the top-driver list. It builds only from customer behaviour that occurred on or before the campaign-issue reference date, exactly matching the data cut used at runtime, so there is no look-ahead leakage. Because it layers audited recency, frequency, points earn/burn, redemption-lag and basket-mix variables on top of the basic RFM set, it gives the model richer behavioural heterogeneity signals than the demographic-heavy baseline.
feature_expected_signal: Held-out Qini should remain ≥326 and uplift-AUC should rise toward or above 0.065, with XAI showing recency, points burn-rate and basket depth among the top drivers while age stays secondary.
judge_narrative: The new model’s held-out Qini AUC dropped from 325.98 to 300.20 and uplift-AUC fell from 0.0589 to 0.0563 versus the prior champion, missing the target improvements (Qini ≥326 and uplift-AUC ≥0.065).
policy_narrative: Targeting the top 5% delivers the strongest positive ROI (28.7%) and a solid incremental lift without dipping into negative returns observed at higher thresholds.

strategy_rationale: Previous best used class_transformation + XGBoost with the same hybrid_safe_semantic_v1 feature recipe. We now keep the audited behavioural features (recency, frequency, points earn/burn, basket mix, etc.) and the safe_history_until_reference temporal cut, but swap the learner to LightGBM to test whether its node-wise growth can capture finer treatment interactions. This directly tests the semantic rationale that behavioural variables—not age—should dominate uplift drivers. Feature semantics: This recipe already proved it can displace age-dominance by supplying audited recency, frequency, points earn/burn, redemption-lag and basket-mix variables, all cut at safe_history_until_reference. That matches the exact cut-off used at runtime, so we eliminate look-ahead leakage while giving the model the richest behavioural heterogeneity signal we have found so far. It therefore aligns with the project goal of testing whether behavioural semantics (not demographics) drive uplift. | Expected: Held-out Qini should reach or exceed 326 (≥ previous 324) and uplift-AUC should remain ≥ 0.060. XAI inspection should show recency, frequency, points burn-rate and basket depth among the top drivers, with age no longer the primary contributor. Expected feature signal: Held-out Qini should stay at or above 326 and uplift-AUC ≥0.060; XAI top drivers should be recency, frequency, points burn-rate and basket depth, with age no longer dominant.
feature_semantics_rationale: This recipe already proved it can displace age-dominance by supplying audited recency, frequency, points earn/burn, redemption-lag and basket-mix variables, all cut at safe_history_until_reference. That matches the exact cut-off used at runtime, so we eliminate look-ahead leakage while giving the model the richest behavioural heterogeneity signal we have found so far. It therefore aligns with the project goal of testing whether behavioural semantics (not demographics) drive uplift.
feature_expected_signal: Held-out Qini should stay at or above 326 and uplift-AUC ≥0.060; XAI top drivers should be recency, frequency, points burn-rate and basket depth, with age no longer dominant.
judge_narrative: The LightGBM model improved held-out Qini AUC from 325.98 to 331.77 (↑5.79) and uplift-AUC from 0.05888 to 0.06149 (↑0.00261), both exceeding the success criteria (Qini ≥326, uplift-AUC ≥0.060).
policy_narrative: The 5% cutoff delivers the highest lift rate (14%) and a strong positive ROI (0.40), while aligning with the elbow threshold to focus on top responders under budget.
